question: Explain the purpose of using packages in Java and describe the two main benefits they provide.
expectedPoints: Packages are used to organize related classes and interfaces into a namespace to avoid naming conflicts.
They provide improved maintainability by grouping related code together, making the project structure clearer.
They control member access through package-level (default) access, allowing classes within the same package to access members that are not explicitly declared public, private, or protected.

question: Describe the difference between public, protected, default (package-private), and private access modifiers in Java. For each, specify which other classes can access a member with that modifier.
expectedPoints: public: Accessible from any other class.
protected: Accessible by classes within the same package and subclasses (even if in a different package).
default (package-private): Accessible only by classes within the same package (no keyword is used).
private: Accessible only within the same class where it is declared.

question: If a class member is declared without an access modifier (default access), from which other classes can it be accessed?
expectedPoints: It can be accessed only by other classes that are defined within the same package.
